skill.name: skool-money-model-strategist
skill.description: Designs and evaluates Skool community monetization models using Hormozi-style offer sequencing, CAC-based stage diagnosis, and deterministic financial calculators.
skill.summary: Build CAC-aware Skool monetization roadmaps with staged pricing, upsells, tiers, and one-time purchase strategies.
skill.author: DaronVee

security_audit.summary: The high static risk score is driven by false positives in markdown documentation and business terminology. Targeted review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or dangerous command execution; the only real risk factor is a local arithmetic helper script used for financial calculations.

low_findings.title: Local Financial Helper Script Execution
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use scripts/math_helpers.py for financial calculations. The script contains deterministic arithmetic helpers and a local __main__ self-test, with no subprocess, network, environment, or file access found during targeted review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is confirmed by skill instructions and reviewed helper code. Risk remains low because execution is local and limited to arithmetic functions.

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory References Are Installation Paths
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings point to ~/.claude installation examples and directory listings. These are user-facing setup instructions and do not read, write, or exfiltrate hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced paths are documentation for skill installation. No code path uses these locations to access secrets or hidden files.

low_findings.title: External Links Are Product and Repository Documentation
low_findings.description: Network findings are hardcoded documentation links such as Claude pages and repository release references. No source file contains code that performs HTTP requests or sends user data to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links are visible documentation references. Repository search found no requests, urllib, sockets, or similar network APIs.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged markdown command examples, URLs, hidden Claude installation paths, and words such as "same" as dangerous patterns. Reviewed contexts show installation documentation and business strategy references, not credential access, reconnaissance, cryptography, or malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show release download instructions, local install paths, and pricing framework prose. They do not perform command execution, network access, cryptographic operations, or credential reads.

content.value_statement: Skool community owners need pricing models that can fund customer acquisition without adding too much complexity. This skill diagnoses the revenue gap and recommends focused monetization mechanisms with setup steps and math checks.
actual_capabilities: Diagnoses a Skool business stage using CAC and first 30-day revenue.
Calculates weighted 30-day cash from monthly, annual, and one-time purchase revenue.
Evaluates tier pricing relationships and flags weak price spacing.
Maps Skool monetization options to attraction, upsell, downsell, and continuity mechanisms.
Creates implementation roadmaps with Skool setup steps, test metrics, and sequencing.
Requires source citations from included reference guides before recommending mechanisms.
limitations: It does not guarantee revenue, conversion rates, or lower churn.
It depends on user-provided CAC, revenue, churn, and conversion data.
It is focused on Skool monetization, not full business strategy or content creation.
Some Skool platform details may need current product verification before implementation.

use_cases.title: Validate a New Skool Offer
use_cases.description: Check whether a planned subscription, tier, or one-time purchase model can cover CAC and support the next growth stage.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Community Revenue
use_cases.description: Review current MRR, paid member counts, and one-time purchase revenue to find the next monetization mechanism to test.

use_cases.title: Design Paid Tier Strategy
use_cases.description: Compare tier prices, upgrade paths, and classroom purchases to produce a staged monetization roadmap for a client.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Diagnosis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to diagnose my Skool money model. My CAC is [amount], monthly price is [amount], annual price is [amount], and my goal is [goal]. Ask for any missing data before recommending changes.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Current Pricing
prompt_templates.scenario: Owner with an active paid community
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate my current Skool pricing against CAC and 30-day cash goals. I have [paid members], [MRR], [monthly price], [annual price], [annual purchase rate], [one-time products], and [one-time take rate].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Tier Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Skool tier strategy for my community. Current offer: [offer]. Audience problem: [problem]. CAC: [amount]. Constraints: [low-touch or high-touch]. Include tier pricing validation and one recommended first mechanism.

prompt_templates.title: Stress Test a Full Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Stress test this Skool monetization model against Hormozi mechanisms and Skool implementation constraints: [model details]. Identify prerequisite gaps, math risks, sequencing risks, and the highest-leverage first test.

output_examples.input: CAC is $250, monthly price is $99, and no one-time purchase exists yet.
output_examples.output: The skill would diagnose the stage, calculate the 30-day cash gap, and recommend one focused mechanism such as an annual upgrade or one-time classroom purchase test.

output_examples.input: A community has three planned tiers at $97, $197, and $497 per month.
output_examples.output: The skill would assess the tier spacing, explain whether the prices create a clear buying decision, and suggest a cleaner decoy or anchor relationship if needed.

output_examples.input: An owner wants to add six monetization ideas at once.
output_examples.output: The skill would reduce the plan to one or two tests, define success metrics, and sequence later mechanisms after the first test produces reliable data.

best_practices: Provide CAC, first 30-day revenue, paid member count, churn, and conversion data before asking for recommendations.
Use the included math helper for calculations and review the displayed assumptions before acting.
Test one mechanism for 30 to 60 days before adding another monetization layer.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for guaranteed conversion rates or revenue predictions.
Do not implement several pricing mechanisms at the same time without a test plan.
Do not use the skill for non-Skool funnels, sales copy, or course content creation.

faq.question: Does this skill run financial calculations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a local Python helper with deterministic functions for stage diagnosis, revenue gaps, MRR projections, and tier validation.

faq.question: Is this only for paid Skool communities?
faq.answer: No. It can analyze free, subscription, freemium, tiered, and one-time payment models.

faq.question: Can it choose the best price for my offer?
faq.answer: It can recommend price structures and tests, but final pricing depends on your market, offer strength, and real conversion data.

faq.question: Does it create sales copy?
faq.answer: No. It provides offer structure, pricing logic, and implementation guidance rather than finished sales pages or emails.

faq.question: Why does it ask for CAC?
faq.answer: CAC is required to determine whether first 30-day revenue can fund acquisition and growth.

faq.question: Can consultants use it for clients?
faq.answer: Yes. Consultants can use it to audit client monetization models, compare tier options, and prepare implementation roadmaps.
